documentation.value: Selects specified variables from the results of matching graph patterns.
One or more variables (e.g. `?name`, `?age`) to project from the result set
A graph pattern containing triple patterns and constraints
Specifies the graph pattern to match against the RDF dataset.
Triple patterns, filters, optionals, and other pattern elements
Restricts solutions to those for which the expression evaluates to true.
A boolean expression, e.g. `?age > 18`, `regex(?name, 'pattern')`, `isURI(?x)`
Extends solutions with bindings from the optional pattern. Solutions are not rejected if the optional does not match.
A graph pattern that is optionally matched
Combines results from two graph patterns (logical OR).
The first alternative graph pattern
The second alternative graph pattern
Groups query solutions by one or more variables for use with aggregate functions.
The variable(s) to group by
Sorts the result set by the given expression(s) in ascending or descending order.
The expression or variable to sort by, optionally wrapped in ASC() or DESC()
Restricts the number of solutions returned.
The maximum number of results to return
Skips the specified number of solutions before returning results.
The number of results to skip
Constructs an RDF graph from a template for each solution to the graph pattern.
A set of triple patterns forming the output graph template
The graph pattern to match
Returns a boolean indicating whether the query pattern has a solution.
The graph pattern to test for existence
Returns an RDF graph that describes the specified resource(s).
A URI or variable identifying the resource to describe
Assigns the value of an expression to a variable within a graph pattern.
The expression to evaluate
The variable to bind the result to
Aggregate function that counts the number of solutions or distinct values.
A variable or `*` to count, optionally with DISTINCT
Aggregate function that returns the sum of numeric values.
A numeric expression or variable to sum
